introSection: Rocket was a steel roller coaster previously located at Furuvik in Gävle, Gävleborgs, Sweden. The ride was a Schwarzkopf Jet Star II, and it previously operated at Alton Towers from 1984 to 5 March 2005 as the Black Hole.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Black Hole was one of six rides added for the 1984 season.

When the ride was at Alton Towers, it was located in a giant canvas-covered building. In 1988, it was modified by BHS to run two-car trains. Previously it ran single cars.

It closed on 5 March 2005, and was removed the following year, however the building containing the ride wasn't removed until early 2012, to make way for The Smiler.

After a renovation from Gerstlauer, the ride reopened at Furuvik as Rocket on 21 May 2011.

Rocket was put up for sale in late 2021. As of late 2025, it remains for sale at €400,000.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Red track and blue supports, used to have black track with track when at Alton Towers.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The ride was modified in 1988 by BHS to support trains with multiple cars. Normally Jet Star II models can only support single car trains.

fullText:
Rocket was a steel roller coaster previously located at Furuvik in Gävle, Gävleborgs, Sweden. The ride was a Schwarzkopf Jet Star II, and it previously operated at Alton Towers from 1984 to 5 March 2005 as the Black Hole.

Black Hole was one of six rides added for the 1984 season.

When the ride was at Alton Towers, it was located in a giant canvas-covered building. In 1988, it was modified by BHS to run two-car trains. Previously it ran single cars.

It closed on 5 March 2005, and was removed the following year, however the building containing the ride wasn't removed until early 2012, to make way for The Smiler.

After a renovation from Gerstlauer, the ride reopened at Furuvik as Rocket on 21 May 2011.

Rocket was put up for sale in late 2021. As of late 2025, it remains for sale at €400,000.



Red track and blue supports, used to have black track with track when at Alton Towers.

The ride was modified in 1988 by BHS to support trains with multiple cars. Normally Jet Star II models can only support single car trains.
